title: 01 Why This Question Matters
thesis: Boundary reorganization in HCI can be structurally visible before stable yearly topic labels emerge.
cautionNote: Interest is in structural reorganization, not hype about prediction.

title: 02 Core Idea
thesis: Persistent loops provide boundary signals; community-aware cycle analysis turns them into interpretable bridge hypotheses.
cautionNote: A loop is not itself a mature topic or causal mechanism.

title: 03 Data and Temporal Construction
thesis: Yearly spaces are built with strict historical cutoffs so validation remains temporally honest.
figureCaption: Figure 1 pipeline with stage-level input/output objects.
cautionNote: Cross-year comparison concerns yearly states, not one fixed object identity.

title: 04 Mathematical Pipeline
thesis: Formulas are mapped directly to operational steps and plain-language interpretations.
figureCaption: Figure 2 loop birth and representative cycle mechanics.
cautionNote: Mathematical definitions specify computation, not causality.

title: 05 Loop Meaning and Bridge Extraction
thesis: Representative cycles and community boundaries define bridge candidates with an explicit, reproducible rule.
figureCaption: Figure 3 community boundary and cross-community edge extraction.
cautionNote: Bridge candidates are ranked hypotheses, not guaranteed future outcomes.

title: 06 Ranking and Temporal Validation
thesis: Ranking quality is judged by whether later-relevant candidates appear near the top of early-year lists.
figureCaption: Figure 4 nDCG@50 explanation and top-heavy evaluation intuition.
cautionNote: Validation is retrospective and out-of-time, not prospective deployment proof.

title: 07 Case Walkthroughs
thesis: Case analysis ties local geometry, cycle structure, community boundaries, and later evidence into claim-bounded interpretations.
cautionNote: Case narratives illustrate plausibility, not inevitability.

title: 08 Claim Boundaries
thesis: Direct outputs, supported interpretations, and non-established claims are explicitly separated.
cautionNote: Do not conflate structural signals with social causation.

title: 09 References and Figure Production Specs
thesis: References are metadata-checked and figure specifications are detailed enough for reproducible implementation.
figureCaption: Verified bibliography and implementation-oriented figure instructions.
cautionNote: Uncertain dataset/protocol details are explicitly listed for confirmation.
